AutoLyap
  • What’s new
  • v0.2.0
  • View page source

v0.2.0

AutoLyap 0.2.0 is a substantial update focused on API clarity, backend flexibility, new documentation, and stronger test coverage.

Highlights

  • Added top-level SolverOptions support to configure solver backends explicitly in analysis calls.

  • Added end-to-end documentation in Quick start.

  • Added worked examples for custom and standard methods: Define your own algorithm: The proximal gradient method and The proximal point method.

  • Refactored problem-class internals into focused modules (base, functions, operators, indices, inclusion_problem) with stricter input validation and clearer error messages.

  • Updated Lyapunov SDP verification/search routines to return certificates in solver results.

  • Added verbosity controls for Lyapunov verification/search routines:

    • IterationIndependent.verify_iteration_independent_Lyapunov

    • IterationIndependent.LinearConvergence.bisection_search_rho

    • IterationDependent.verify_iteration_dependent_Lyapunov

  • Added runtime diagnostic summaries covering:

    • nonnegativity-constrained scalars,

    • PSD-constrained matrices (minimum-eigenvalue checks),

    • equality constraints (max_abs_residual, l2_residual).

Breaking changes

  • Interpolation index tokens were renamed:

    • i<j -> r1<r2

    • i!=j -> r1!=r2

    • i -> r1

    • i!=star -> r1!=star

Migration from 0.1.0

  1. Update interpolation-index strings in custom conditions and related code.

  2. If needed, switch to explicit backend configuration with SolverOptions.

  3. Re-run your analyses and tests to confirm expected rates/certificates.

Example index-token update:

from autolyap.problemclass import InterpolationIndices

# Old (0.1.0)
# idx = InterpolationIndices("i!=j")

# New (0.2.0)
idx = InterpolationIndices("r1!=r2")

Example backend configuration:

from autolyap import SolverOptions

solver_options = SolverOptions(backend="mosek_fusion")
# or:
# solver_options = SolverOptions(backend="cvxpy", cvxpy_solver="CLARABEL")

Documentation and quality improvements

  • Introduced documentation navigation, API organization, and quick-start flow.

  • Updated diagnostics wording to use neutral phrasing when no violations are present (for example, “smallest … (nonnegative)”).

  • Expanded test coverage for algorithms, convergence checks, backends, and problem-class validation.

  • Added CI workflow coverage for automated testing.
